arts.ttl: Manchester United eyeing Atalanta midfielder Ederson for January transfer window
arts.sum: Manchester United are preparing to make a strategic move in the upcoming January transfer window, with Atalanta midfielder Ederson emerging as a prime target for the club. Sky Sport Germany report that the 25-year-old Brazilian has caught the attention of new manager Ruben Amorim as he seeks to strengthen the team’s midfield options. Reports suggest that United are seriously considering Ederson, who has been a standout performer for Atalanta. The midfielder has been a consistent presence in the starting lineup, demonstrating versatility by playing in central and defensive midfield roles. arts.ttl: Atalanta chasing top spot in Parma
arts.sum: Atalanta have won each of their last six Serie A matches, while the only time they have had a longer run in the competition was between February and July 2020 (nine in that case) – among the teams in the Big-5 European Leagues this season, only Barcelona have had a run of seven straight wins. In Saturday’s 20:45 CET kick-off, Parma will be looking for three big points in the relegation fight when they face an Atalanta side who have won their last six matches

arts.ttl: Juventus Eye Zirkzee as Napoli and Atalanta Compete for Raspadori
Giorgio Scalvini’s returns to Atalanta squad for Parma match
arts.sum: In a significant development for Atalanta, young defender Giorgio Scalvini has been included in the squad for the upcoming match against Parma, marking his return after a lengthy recovery from a serious knee injury. This comeback comes just over 170 days since he suffered a torn anterior cruciate ligament during a match against Fiorentina on June 2, 2024. Scalvini’s recovery is notable not only for its speed but also for its potential impact on Atalanta’s defensive lineup as they continue their campaign in Serie A. arts.ttl: Serie A | Parma v Atalanta: Match Preview
arts.sum: Serie A’s surprise title contenders Atalanta travel to face newly-promoted Parma on Saturday, looking to extend their impressive winning streak and keep pace at the top of the table. The Bergamo-based club have been in scintillating form, winning their last six league matches to surge into second place, just one point off the summit. Gian Piero Gasperini’s men have been simply irresistible in recent weeks, sweeping aside all before them with a potent mix of attacking flair and defensive solidity.
